statements.rationale: Operational noise suppression; dbt does not require this setting.

statements.sql: DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX); DECLARE @target NVARCHAR(128) = 'silver.ExecConcatTarget'; DECLARE @src NVARCHAR(128) = 'bronze.Product'
statements.rationale: Variable declarations used only to build the dynamic SQL string; no transformation logic.

statements.sql: SET @sql = 'INSERT INTO ' + @target + ' (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName) ' + 'SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName ' + 'FROM ' + @src
statements.rationale: String concatenation that constructs the dynamic SQL; the logic lives in the resolved EXEC statement, not here.

statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.ExecConcatTarget (ProductAlternateKey, EnglishProductName) SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)), ProductName FROM bronze.Product
statements.rationale: Dynamic SQL is built with concatenation before EXEC, so LLM-assisted resolution is required to recover the final statement.
